Using WOWJS in a Vue CLI Project
I've been building a small project lately with Vue CLI, so here's a quick note on how to use WOWJS inside a Vue CLI app.
WOWJS is usually paired with Animate.css. First, head into your project directory and run:
language-cmd shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
(the entry point) and add these two lines:
language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
// This line is the key — without it, WOW won't work properly.
Now you can use it in your Vue components. Try dropping this into any component to test:
language-html shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
If you want to tweak WOW's delay, duration, offset, or iteration count:
WOW is basically controlled with the

data-wow-duration: animation duration
data-wow-delay: animation delay
data-wow-offset: distance to trigger the animation
data-wow-iteration: number of times to repeat (
If you want to switch animation libraries, just tweak the WOW config in
Class of the element to animate (default
How far into the viewport before the animation triggers (typically near the middle of the screen)
Whether to run animations on mobile
Whether async-loaded content also triggers animations
